author: Saiki77
description: Surface notes most semantically similar to the one you're reading in a left-sidebar panel, powered by a small multilingual embedding model that runs entirely on your machine. Private and offline. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Saiki77
manifest.description: Surface notes most semantically similar to the one you're reading in a left-sidebar panel, powered by a small multilingual embedding model that runs entirely on your machine. Private and offline.
manifest.name: Smart Related Notes

name: Smart Related Notes

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Streamlined install + community-review fixes

Releases now ship exactly the three files Obsidian installs (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) — the runtime zip is gone. Instead, the plugin **downloads the pinned ONNX runtime once (~24 MB) into its own folder on first use** and serves it locally from then on, so **every** install method (community directory, BRAT, manual) now works offline after first run — previously only the full-zip install did.

- The cached runtime is **integrity-checked against byte sizes pinned at build time**: interrupted downloads, leftovers from older installs, or a proxy serving garbage are detected and cleanly re-downloaded instead of causing cryptic startup errors.
- Runtime download tries jsdelivr first, then unpkg — one blocked CDN no longer breaks first-run setup.
- Existing installs that already have the runtime folder (e.g. from the 2.1.x zips) keep using it — no re-download.
- Internal changes for the community-plugin automated review (no `fetch` in plugin code; assets are read through the vault API / `requestUrl`).

See [2.1.1](https://github.com/Saiki77/smart-related-notes/releases/tag/2.1.1) and [2.1.0](https://github.com/Saiki77/smart-related-notes/releases/tag/2.1.0) for the idle memory-release feature this builds on.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## More aggressive memory release

New choices for **"Unload model when idle"**:

- **Right after use (30 seconds)** — the embedding engine is torn down half a minute after its last piece of work, so it effectively only exists *during* indexing/search bursts.
- **After 1 minute**

30 seconds is the floor rather than "instant" because a respawn costs a few seconds of background CPU and note edits are re-indexed on a 20-second pause — anything shorter would tear the engine down between consecutive edits and immediately respawn it.

With the aggressive settings, the first search or `[[` suggestion after a pause shows the keyword/recency fallback for a few seconds while the engine reloads (the panel says "Loading the embedding model…"), then results upgrade.

See [2.1.0](https://github.com/Saiki77/smart-related-notes/releases/tag/2.1.0) for the underlying change (engine in a terminable worker, memory actually returned to the OS, offline/CDN fix, detailed error messages).

**Installing manually?** Use `smart-related-notes.zip` (it includes the bundled ONNX runtime). Installs of just `main.js` fall back to the CDN.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Memory: the plugin now frees its RAM when idle

The embedding engine (transformers.js + ONNX runtime) now runs in its own worker and is **shut down after 15 minutes without indexing or searching** (configurable via the new **"Unload model when idle"** setting), returning its memory — previously hundreds of MB to several GB held until Obsidian quit — to the system. It reloads automatically in a few seconds on next use, with no re-download. Ranking when switching notes never needs the engine and is unaffected.

Also in this release:

- **Fixed: model/device/speed changes leaked memory** — each change stacked another copy of the engine's memory on top of the old one.
- **Fixed: offline use & CDN dependency** — 2.0.x silently ignored its bundled ONNX runtime and always fetched it from jsdelivr's CDN. Full installs now use the shipped copy (works offline; fixes *"could not load the embedding model"* on networks that block the CDN).
- **Fixed: changing the model mid-index** could save an index mixing old- and new-model vectors; the rebuild now restarts cleanly.
- **Better errors**: model-load failures now say *what* failed (ONNX runtime fetch vs. model download, with the URL/model named) right in the notice — please include it when reporting issues.
- Search shows "Loading the embedding model…" while the engine warms up instead of waiting silently; smoother typing during rebuilds (inference left the main thread).

**Installing manually?** Use `smart-related-notes.zip` (it includes the bundled ONNX runtime). Installs of just `main.js` fall back to the CDN.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.19.0-beta.3: close first-run gate leaks (flushDirty, [[ suggester) + cap/doc fixes

Adversarial review (verdict fix-then-ship; hybrid verified correct, EOS-truncation false
positive dropped). Fixes:
- HIGH: flushDirty() now returns early (clears dirty) while !modelChosen — editing a note
  on a fresh gated install no longer silently downloads the default model.
- HIGH: link-suggester computeContextVec() early-returns while !modelChosen — typing [[
  no longer pulls model weights before a model is picked (falls back to recency).
- LOW: rebuildIndex() gated with a Notice; WHOLE_NOTE_CHARS 14000->10000 with a corrected
  comment (no tokenizer truncation on the single-input path); whole-note cap now counts
  IDEAS (parity with MiniLM's body-chunk cap).
releases.name: 1.19.0-beta.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.19.0-beta.2: jina-v5 opt-in (whole-note + idea hybrid), first-run gate, model manager

- embeddings.ts: per-model ModelSpec (prefix/pooling/wholeNote); jina-v5-nano uses a
  'Document: ' prefix + LAST-TOKEN pooling (single-input, no padding) via embed/embedBatch.
- index-store.ts: whole-note + idea-unit hybrid (chunkNoteWholeNote). For jina the note
  MEAN is one embed of the whole note (its winning signal) and each IDEA is embedded
  whole as a stored chunk (idea extractor preserved, fed by jina). assembleEntry splits
  the isMean chunk off as meanVector; storage is dimension-agnostic (384 MiniLM / 768 jina).
  MiniLM path unchanged.
- main.ts: jina in MODEL_OPTIONS (opt-in, ~250MB, CC-BY-NC labelled); FIRST-RUN GATE
  (modelChosen: fresh installs don't index until a model is picked; existing installs
  migrated to chosen); settings intro blurb; MODEL MANAGER (list + remove cached models).
- Model switch re-embeds via the existing modelId/dims invalidation (no mixed-dim mixing).
- Build + lint green. In-Obsidian jina behavior (pooling/whole-note ranking) is the test.
releases.name: 1.19.0-beta.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ci: verify the asyncify wasm assets (v4) instead of the removed jsep ones
releases.name: 1.19.0-beta.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.18.0: isolated areas (self-contained tag-namespace partitions)

A new "Isolated areas (tag namespaces)" setting lets you activate areas by tag
namespace (one per line, e.g. goa). A note whose tags include an activated namespace
(matching goa AND goa/character) belongs to that area; ranking then partitions the
vault:

- A note in an isolated area only relates to notes in the SAME area, and never appears
  in the cards of any note outside it (a self-contained novel/world stays self-
  contained -- "doesn't appear anywhere else").
- Notes in no activated area share one common pool.
- Tag suggestions scope for free: an isolated note's neighbours are all same-area, so
  e.g. goa/* tags are only ever suggested to goa notes, never to math/study notes.

Implementation: rank()'s Stage-1 shortlist drops candidates failing areaMatch(); area =
top-level tag namespace intersected with options.isolatedAreas. noteAreas()/areaMatch()
helpers; computeInsights (links + tag suggestions) inherits the filter via rank().
Rank-time only -> NO re-embed; changing the setting re-ranks live.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.17.0: semantic tag suggestions (the plugin finds missing tags itself)

Adds a "Suggested tags" section to the Vault insights report: for each note, the tags
a MAJORITY of its semantic neighbours carry but it lacks -- restricted to DISCRIMINATIVE
tags (document-frequency between MIN_TAG_DF and 50% of notes, so a near-universal tag
like "goa" is never proposed) and to notes with enough tagged neighbours
(MIN_TAG_NEIGHBORS) to be trustworthy. Pure label-propagation over the existing
similarity graph -- no rules, no body heuristics.

Validated live on the user's vault: it independently inferred Whispering Shadow and
Vulcan -> #goa/character (10/20 neighbours each) from similarity to the already-tagged
character cluster, and math notes clustering to #bio/#analysis; the min-neighbours guard
drops sparse-note noise (e.g. a 1/2-neighbour false positive).

IndexStore.computeInsights now also returns suggestedTags; rendered in the report. No
index format change -> no re-embed.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.16.0: linked-notes (MOC) mode in the panel

A new link icon in the panel header toggles "linked notes" mode: instead of the notes
most SIMILAR to the active note, the cards show the notes the active note LINKS TO --
i.e. a map-of-content's members. Closes the category gap from the other side: open
"GoA Characters" and the panel lists every character it links to, as clickable cards.

- view.ts: linksMode toggle (mutually exclusive with search), renderLinks() reads
  metadataCache.resolvedLinks for the active note and renders resolved md targets as
  "Linked" cards; empty state when the note links to nothing.
- Active toggle reads as accent (styles.css). No index change -> no re-embed.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.15.0: Vault insights command (link-building + graph hygiene)

A new command "Vault insights (suggested links, orphans, duplicates)" generates a
report note over the whole vault from the live semantic index -- aimed at growing a
sparse link graph and cleanup:

- Suggested links: strongest related-but-UNLINKED pairs (>= 0.5 similarity, top 50),
  the fastest way to connect notes that belong together.
- Orphan notes: notes with no resolved links in or out, each paired with its closest
  relative as a starting point.
- Possibly duplicate: near-identical pairs (>= 0.68), merge/cross-link candidates.
- Stale: oldest-edited notes.

IndexStore.computeInsights(ignore) reuses the same rank() the panel uses; the report
note ignores ITSELF as a link source so re-runs stay accurate. Thresholds tuned to the
observed score distribution (a real 113-note vault: 48 orphans, top unlinked pairs
0.64-0.75). No index format change -> no re-embed. Rank-time only.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.14.0: embed note tags + aliases (restore the frontmatter signal)

Chunking strips YAML frontmatter, which silently DISCARDED each note's tags and
aliases -- exactly the "this is a GoA character" context the user expects search and
related-notes to use. Now the title chunk's EMBED input folds in aliases + tags
(display text stays the bare basename), via the existing normalizeTag/addFrontmatterTags
helpers plus a new addAliases. So a note matches on its alternate names and category
tags, not just its prose.

Note on behavior: a tag present on nearly every note (e.g. "goa") is absorbed into the
corpus centroid and centered out at rank time, so the lift comes from DISTINCTIVE tags
and aliases; a universal tag is neutral, not harmful. A per-type tag (e.g. #character)
would make that category razor-sharp.

INDEX_VERSION 7->8 (one-time re-embed).

Also adds bench/ab-eval.mjs: an offline link-recall A/B harness (ground truth = the
user's wikilinks). Result on the user's real vault (24 linked notes): MiniLM/windows+
biMax (current) R@10=0.429 BEATS BGE-M3/windows 0.351 and BGE-M3/whole-note 0.212 --
so the 568MB long-context model is a measurable REGRESSION here, and the small
paraphrase model + MaxSim-over-windows stays the right call. Path A confirmed by data.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.13.0: better semantic search — best-passage match + title boost

Search ranked notes purely by the note AVERAGE, so a query like "goa character"
surfaced notes whose whole topic is "characters" (list/idea notes) and buried both the
individual character notes and even the note literally named "GoA Characters". Two
fixes, both rank-time only (NO re-embed, no index change):

- Best-passage re-rank: after the mean-cosine shortlist, score each candidate by the
  best-matching passage (max cosine of the query to any of the note's chunk vectors),
  blended 0.4 mean / 0.6 passage. A note that discusses the query in ONE section now
  surfaces even if its overall gist is something else.
- Lexical title/path boost: + up to 0.25 for query words present in the note path, so
  searching "goa characters" puts the note literally named that near the top instead
  of below an unrelated template.

Note: a category query ("list all X") is still bounded by semantic similarity — to
enumerate members of a MOC, following its outgoing links is the structural answer
(a candidate future mode).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.12.0: idea-level grouping + idea-aware ranking (semantic chunking)

Paragraph boundaries are a poor unit for "an idea" (a paragraph is often one
sentence). This groups adjacent passages into coherent IDEAS (~200-500 words) and
blends idea-level matching into ranking, so a note related by one whole idea spanning
several paragraphs surfaces, not just on a single lucky passage hit.

- assignIdeas() (index-store.ts): groups the body windows into ideas on the FULL
  pre-cap stream (true text adjacency) at heading transitions + adaptive lexical-
  cohesion valleys, bounded by size rails (MAX_IDEA_WINDOWS) and a word floor
  (MIN_IDEA_WORDS, coalesced by mergeSmallIdeas so bullet-heavy notes reach the target
  instead of leaving 40-word fragments). Atomic-note guard keeps short math/technical
  notes as one idea. Pure text -> deterministic + model-independent (ideas don't shift
  when the model is swapped, keeping a future model A/B clean).
- Each note persists a small ideaOf[] (one idea id per row) in the JSON manifest only
  -- the binary blob layout is UNCHANGED. ideaMaxSim() aggregates the centered window
  rows into per-idea vectors and runs the existing directionalMax (same geometry as
  biMax, title idea weighted). rank() blends ((1-w)*biMax + w*ideaMaxSim).
- "Idea influence" w is a LIVE rank-time knob (default 0.3): it re-ranks instantly
  with NO re-embed, so it can be A/B'd by sliding 0..0.6. 0 == byte-identical to v1.11.
- INDEX_VERSION 6->7 (one-time re-embed to populate ideaOf).

Verified: research+design+adversarial workflow (verdict fix-then-ship; fixes applied)
plus a live run of the algorithm against the user's real notes (atomic notes -> 1
idea; long prose -> ~200-360-word ideas; no fragments). A higher-quality long-context
model (BGE-M3) remains a measured A/B before adoption -- this idea layer is the shared
foundation either way.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.11.1: clean heading breadcrumbs in structure-aware chunking

Live verification (running the new chunker against real notes in Obsidian's console)
confirmed the rewrite works end to end — whole-note coverage with the longest notes
previously losing ~96% of their content to the 1500-char cap, real headings detected
(19 sections in one note), every chunk within the token budget (0 over 480). It also
surfaced one cosmetic issue, fixed here:

- splitIntoSections now strips inline markdown (**bold**, _italic_, `code`, [[links]])
  from heading text, so a heading like `# **Stage A — Ethereal Artificer:**` yields the
  breadcrumb "Stage A — Ethereal Artificer:" instead of carrying the literal ** markers
  into the heading-context embed prefix. Cleaner context for the embedder.
- INDEX_VERSION 5 -> 6 so any index already built on 1.11.0 re-embeds once with the
  cleaned prefixes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.11.0: whole-note structure-aware chunking (foundational embedding overhaul)

Notes are no longer truncated to 1500 chars and chunked by blind sentence windows.
The whole note is now split at LOGICAL boundaries into coherent idea-chunks, so a note
is an overall embedding PLUS a set of section-level chunks other notes/queries align
with section-by-section.

- splitIntoSections(): parse the raw note into sections at ATX headings (code-fence +
  frontmatter aware), carrying a heading breadcrumb; headings are the primary idea
  boundary, paragraphs the secondary one. A window never crosses a section/paragraph.
- splitToBudget(): hard char guard (MAX_CHUNK_CHARS=480 ≈ 120 tokens) splitting any
  window at sentence then whitespace boundaries so the model never silently truncates
  a chunk (EN + DE). TARGET_WORDS 60->80.
- Whole-note coverage: removed embedCharLimit truncation entirely; chunk-count cap
  16->48 (adaptive tiers raised), over-cap keeps every section's first window.
- Heading context (new setting, default on): the first chunk of each section embeds
  with a "Note > H1 > H2:" breadcrumb prefix (embed input only; raw text kept for
  snippets), the LLM-free contextual-retrieval trick, scoped to avoid embedding
  collapse. The window is clamped so prefix+window stays within the token budget.
- INDEX_VERSION 4->5: one-time full re-embed on update. meanVector + biMax unchanged
  (better inputs). Both build() and embedFile() embed via a shared chunkEmbedInput()
  helper so the full and incremental paths can't diverge.

Verified by a research-backed design pass + an adversarial review (fixed a high-sev
incremental-path regression and a prefix-budget truncation before shipping).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.10.0: semantic search box in the panel

A magnifier in the panel header (next to the rebuild icon) opens a search box that
ranks the whole vault by meaning against a typed query (e.g. "goa characters",
"proofwork"), on the same mean-centered scale as the related-notes cards.

- store.rankByQuery(query): embeds the query, centers it with the corpus centroid,
  ranks entries by cosine to their centered means (floor = min(minSimilarity, 0.12),
  topK). Keyword fallback (query tokens vs note paths, excludes respected) when the
  engine can't embed or the index is empty, and on an embedding-dim mismatch.
- view: toggle search input; debounced; async results guarded against stale runs;
  render() yields to an active search; debouncers cancelled on close.

Verified by an adversarial review (verdict: ship).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.9.1: fix cards showing through the panel header while scrolling

The header was position:sticky inside a single scrolling view, which let cards
render over/through it during scroll momentum. Switch to a flex column: a fixed
header (flex:0 0 auto) plus the card list as its own scroll area
(flex:1; min-height:0; overflow-y:auto), so cards can never paint outside the list.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.9.0: recent notes in the empty panel + link-suggestion folder exclusion

- When no note is open, the panel now lists recent notes (recently opened, falling
  back to recently modified) as clickable cards instead of "No active note."
- New setting "Folders excluded from link suggestions": notes in these folders stay
  indexed and ranked in the panel but are never offered as inline [[link]] targets.
  The TitleIndex now skips the union of the index-excluded folders and this list, so
  "Excluded folders" also implies no link suggestions (an excluded folder is fully
  excluded). Changing either list marks the TitleIndex dirty so the glow updates.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.8.5: docs-only release (remove em dashes from the README)

No code changes; version bump to ship the README formatting cleanup.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.8.4: rename plugin id + repo to smart-related-notes

The plugin id was `related-notes`, which is already taken by another community
plugin of the same id ("Related Notes" by mrboxtobox). Obsidian requires globally
unique plugin ids, so the directory submission was rejected with "An entry with this
ID already exists." The display name "Smart Related Notes" was never the problem.

Rename the id and the GitHub repo to `smart-related-notes` (display name unchanged).
Also updated to match: the runtime plugin-folder path (self-hosted wasm lookup), the
sidebar view type (avoids clashing if both plugins are installed), the release zip /
packaged folder, the package name, and the manual-install docs. Internal console log
prefixes and CSS class names are intentionally left as-is (no conflict).

Existing BRAT installs should be removed and re-added from Saiki77/smart-related-notes;
because the plugin folder changes, settings and the index rebuild fresh.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.8.3: revert auto->WebGPU (memory crash) + add Indexing speed setting

The 70GB memory crash returned: 1.7.0 made "auto" prefer WebGPU again, and onnxruntime
-web's WebGPU backend still accumulates GPU/unified memory across reindexes (and plugin
reloads) until Obsidian dies — per-pass tensor disposal wasn't enough. Revert: "auto"
(and "wasm") resolve to multi-threaded WASM, which is memory-stable (verified flat at
~9GB across 3 back-to-back reindexes, no growth). WebGPU stays available only as an
EXPLICIT pin, with a clear warning in the setting + README.

Multi-threaded WASM is fast (~28s) but its shared heap holds several GB while loaded,
and that cost is roughly the same at 4 or 8 threads (it's the model's working set, not
per-thread) — only single-threaded is light (~2GB) but slow (~2.5min). Since there's no
fast-AND-light option, expose the trade-off: a new "Indexing speed" setting
(Light = 1 thread / Balanced = ~cores/3 / Fast = cores-2 capped 8), default Balanced,
wired via setEmbedThreads(); changing it recreates the engine (configureEnv re-applies
numThreads) and rebuilds, exactly like a device change. Incremental per-note updates
stay fast at any setting. No INDEX_VERSION change.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.8.2: glow gate — only suppress off-topic CONTENT-RICH notes, let stubs glow

1.8.1 gated ALL empty stubs (glow only if structurally tied), which left notes with
NOTHING highlighted — e.g. an on-topic but empty "LLM" stub stopped glowing in an
essay about LLMs. Now that off-topic notes with real content (a math "Analysis" full
of calculus) are caught by the centered semantic test, the blunt stub rule is no
longer needed.

New glowAllowed: gate ONLY a content-rich target (chunkCount > 1) that is both
off-topic (centered similarity < GLOW_CONTEXT_FLOOR) AND not structurally tied (no
link either direction, no shared tag). Everything else glows — empty stubs (concept
placeholders to link/build out), on-topic notes, and linked/tagged notes. Extracted
the structural test into structurallyTied(). No re-embed.

Verified live: in the security essay, "analysis" (now a content-rich math note) stays
gated, while "llm" (empty stub) glows again — highlighting is back without the
off-topic noise.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.8.1: context-gate the glow — don't link a common word where it doesn't fit

The inline glow linked any exact title match, so "analysis" in a security note glowed
the unrelated math "Analysis" note. Gate each glow (and Link-all / auto-link) by
whether the target fits the active note's CONTEXT, via IndexStore.glowAllowed:
  - Content-rich target: keep only when its centered (topical) similarity to the
    active note clears GLOW_CONTEXT_FLOOR (0.15) — reuses the 1.8.0 mean-centering, so
    an off-topic note scores ~0 and is dropped.
  - Empty stub target: it carries no topic to judge (the title may be an ambiguous
    word), so glow only when there's an explicit structural tie — a link either
    direction or a shared tag. An isolated empty stub (bare "Analysis") has none, so it
    no longer glows off-topic.
Wired via glowBridge.contextGate (set from main.ts), checked in buildGlow and
detectMentions after resolve. Verified live: "analysis" no longer underlines in the
security note (its math target is an isolated empty stub). Note: this also gates other
isolated empty stubs (e.g. an empty "LLM" note) until they gain content or a link —
inherent, since an empty note's topic can't be inferred; it self-heals as notes mature.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.8.0: topic-focused ranking — mean-centering to remove the anisotropy noise floor

Unrelated notes scored a high baseline (~40-50%) purely from embedding-space
anisotropy: every note shares a common direction, so cosine never approaches 0 even
for unrelated topics (a biology note matched a security essay at 41%). Researched the
literature (Mu & Viswanath all-but-the-top, Su et al. BERT-whitening, BERTopic
c-TF-IDF, Hämmerl et al.) and MEASURED it live on the vault: mean anisotropy 0.494,
and simple common-mean removal drives it to -0.037 while keeping real matches high
(security-vs-Proofwork 0.86, security-vs-Bio 0.52 -> -0.06).

Implementation (src/vector-math.ts + src/index-store.ts): compute the corpus centroid
(L2-normalized mean of all note mean-vectors) whenever entries change, and subtract it
(+ re-normalize) from BOTH the Stage-1 mean vectors and the Stage-2 chunk vectors (the
DequantCache centers rows on dequant; centered means feed the stub-grounding path).
Centroid recomputed on load/build/update/remove; no re-embed (reads stored vectors).
Simple common-mean removal is the safe form of isotropy correction — on an already-
isotropic corpus the centroid norm is tiny, so it's a near-no-op, never harmful.

Centering removes the floor, so scores now sit on a lower, floor-free scale: default
minSimilarity 0.45 -> 0.2 (+ profiles), Stage-1 coarse floor goes slightly negative
when centered to preserve recall, and a one-time settings migration lowers any pre-
1.8.0 minSimilarity onto the new scale. Verified live on the security essay: panel went
from 10 noisy results (math/bio stubs at 40-52%) to a clean Proofwork 51 / Projects 35
/ UNI Themen 23, with Bio and all unrelated stubs gone.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.7.2: glow style — slim blue underline instead of a highlight

The inline glow used a filled highlight background + accent box-shadow, which is
visually heavy. Replace it with a slim 1px blue underline (var(--color-blue),
3px offset; 2px on hover) and no background — a subtle, non-distracting hint that a
concept has a note, and visually distinct from a real wikilink (which the theme
colours differently). Verified live.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.7.1: content-confidence — stop near-empty notes ranking high on unrelated notes

Empty/stub notes surfaced at ~40-52% under unrelated notes (e.g. the math stubs
"Ableiten", "Integrieren", "Lineare Algebra" under an AI-security essay). Root cause
is embedding-space anisotropy: a short/empty note embeds to a non-distinctive vector
near the corpus centroid, so it scores a moderate ~0.5 cosine against EVERYTHING. The
v1.4.1 title-vs-mean stub grounding wasn't enough — even the grounded title cosine is
~0.5 for an unrelated one-word title.

Fix: scale the SEMANTIC score by a content-confidence factor derived from the note's
body size (chunkTexts when summaries are on, else body chunk count) — full trust at
CONFIDENT_BODY_CHARS (220), down to MIN_CONTENT_CONFIDENCE (0.4) for a title-only
note. Applied to the semantic part only and SKIPPED for directly-linked notes, so a
note you explicitly linked (e.g. an on-topic "LLM" stub) still surfaces via its link
while spurious unlinked stubs drop below minSimilarity.

No re-embed needed (reads existing chunkTexts/chunkCount). Verified live: math stubs
dropped out, content notes unchanged (Proofwork 61/Projects 53/Bio 41), linked-but-
empty LLM kept at 43%.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.7.0: GPU acceleration + multi-threaded WASM — reindex from minutes to seconds

Indexing was single-threaded WASM on one core with the Best/mpnet model, so a full
reindex took minutes and felt laggy on an otherwise idle, powerful machine. Measured
live on a 14-core Mac with an Apple GPU (per-text on real note chunks):

  single-thread WASM (old)   ~130ms  -> full reindex ~2.5-3 min
  multi-threaded WASM         ~22ms  -> full reindex ~28s
  WebGPU (Apple GPU)           ~6ms  -> full reindex ~6.5s

Three changes:

1. MULTI-THREADED WASM. The renderer isn't cross-origin isolated, but Electron
   exposes SharedArrayBuffer anyway, so ort-web threaded WASM runs. configureEnv now
   sets numThreads = min(cores-2, 8) instead of 1 (~6x faster; verified threads
   actually engage). This is the CPU fallback.

2. WebGPU LEAK FIXED, so "auto" prefers the GPU again. The old leak (a reindex
   ballooned to ~70GB, the reason 1.6.2 forced auto->wasm) is gone: embedBatch now
   runs fixed-size sub-batches AND disposes each output tensor, releasing GPU buffers
   every pass. Verified live: 3 back-to-back reindexes held memory flat (~10GB,
   trending down via GC), zero growth. "auto" => webgpu when an adapter is present,
   else multi-threaded wasm.

3. dispose() on every embed output (the leak fix above; harmless on WASM).

The 1.6.4 MessageChannel yield + 1.6.3 sub-batching/label-gating/persist-mutex all
remain. ort/glue loading untouched. Settings + README copy updated. No INDEX_VERSION
change. Validated end to end in the live vault: auto picks webgpu, full reindex ~6.5s,
UI responsive, memory stable.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.6.5: fix glow click — resolve by coordinates, convert via the CM6 view directly

Clicking a glowing mention never converted it to a link. Two causes:
- The mousedown guard checked e.target.classList.contains("srn-glow"), but in live
  preview the glow <span> is routinely wrapped by (or wraps) other CM/Obsidian spans,
  so the clicked node is usually a CHILD without the glow class — the check failed and
  the handler bailed immediately.
- Insertion round-tripped through the Obsidian editor (offsetToPos translation + an
  active-editor lookup), each a silent-failure point.

Fix: resolve the click to a document offset from pointer COORDINATES
(view.posAtCoords) instead of e.target, look up the glow range covering that offset,
re-validate the surface against the live doc, and apply the wikilink with the CM6
view DIRECTLY (view.dispatch with the same offsets, cursor placed after the link).
Left-click only. No model/ort/index changes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.6.4: fix reindex crawling for minutes — yield via MessageChannel, not setTimeout

v1.6.3 made embedBatch yield between sub-batches via window.setTimeout(0). Chromium
clamps nested setTimeout to >=4ms and throttles timers to ~1/second when the window
is unfocused or the renderer is busy (this vault also runs Dataview, Excalidraw,
Calendar, Charts). A reindex does many yields, so on a loaded window the timer
throttling turned a few-second index into MINUTES of waiting — CPU and RAM idle, the
whole app laggy. Classic "stuck on throttled timers" signature.

Fix: a shared src/async-yield.ts yieldToUI() built on a MessageChannel message — a
macrotask that still lets the renderer paint/handle input but is NOT subject to
setTimeout's clamping/throttling (the technique React's scheduler uses). Both
embedBatch (sub-batch yields) and build() (between-batch yields) now use it. embedBatch
also yields on a ~20ms TIME BUDGET rather than after every sub-batch, so a fast machine
yields rarely (near full throughput) while a slow one stays responsive. Vector order/
values unchanged (ranking identical); ort/device loading untouched; no INDEX_VERSION
change.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.6.3: responsive reindex, defer labels off the load path, fix persist ENOENT race

After v1.6.2 moved indexing to WASM (CPU, main thread), three issues surfaced live:

1. Reindex FROZE the UI. embedBatch ran one pipe() over a whole outer batch
   (~130+ chunk texts) — a single uninterruptible synchronous WASM pass of several
   seconds. Now embedBatch sub-batches inputs in slices of EMBED_SUB_BATCH (12) and
   yields a macrotask between slices, so the renderer keeps painting. Rows are
   concatenated in input order, so the returned vectors are byte-identical and
   ranking output is unchanged (embed()/1-text batches skip the extra yield).

2. Lazy keyphrase labels embedded DURING load/reindex (render -> getSummary ->
   scheduleLabel -> drainLabels -> embedBatch -> model init), piling blocking
   inference onto the reindex. Added labelsAllowed() — false while building/loading
   or a vector write is in flight — gating both scheduleLabel (skips queueing) and
   drainLabels (defers + re-arms). Labels resume once indexing settles; getSummary
   still returns its snippet fallback immediately.

3. Uncaught ENOENT: rename 'index.json.tmp' -> 'index.json'. persist() and the
   label drain's persistManifestOnly() could run concurrently, both writing the same
   index.json.tmp; the first swapInto renamed it away, the second threw on a missing
   tmp — and it was an uncaught rejection. All persists now funnel through a
   persistChain mutex (persistSerial) with .catch logging, so only one write+swap
   runs at a time and a failure never rejects unhandled. swapInto also no-ops if tmp
   is already gone (defence-in-depth).

ort/wasm/glue/device loading untouched. No INDEX_VERSION change. Build + lint green.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.6.2: fix massive memory leak on reindex — "auto" no longer uses WebGPU

A full reindex spiked to ~70GB of RAM and crashed Obsidian. Root cause: the
"auto" compute device (the default) preferred WebGPU whenever an adapter was
present, and onnxruntime-web's WebGPU backend accumulates GPU buffers across the
many embed() calls of a (re)index. On a unified-memory Mac that GPU memory IS
system RAM, so indexing a whole vault ballooned without bound until the process
died — disproportionate to the tiny vault, the signature of per-call accumulation
rather than data size.

Fix: "auto" now resolves to the memory-stable WASM (CPU) backend, same as "wasm".
WebGPU stays available only as an EXPLICIT pin for users who accept the cost and
mainly want single-query speed; the setting + README now warn about the reindex
memory spike. WASM is plenty fast for note-sized inputs and reuses its heap across
calls, so a reindex stays flat.

No INDEX_VERSION change; the reindex simply runs on WASM now.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.6.1: fix index persist — adapter.rename throws over an existing destination

1.6.0's binary persist wrote to .tmp then `adapter.rename(tmp, dest)`, but
Obsidian's adapter.rename throws "Destination file already exists!" when dest
exists. So the FIRST write (after the v4 rebuild discarded the old index) worked,
but every later persist failed — breaking lazy-label persistence AND incremental
index saves on edit. Caught live in the vault.

Fix: a swapInto(tmp, dest) helper that removes dest before renaming (the complete
new data is already in tmp; load()'s totalBytes check self-heals the brief
remove→rename window). Used by both persist() and persistManifestOnly().

Verified live: reload + switch through several notes → no "already exists" error,
lazy labels persist, rankings intact. Benchmark still bit-identical (max diff 0).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.6.0: scale to 1000+ notes — lazy dequant, binary index, lazy labels (ranking unchanged)

Ranking latency was already flat via the 2-stage funnel; this pass kills the
at-rest vector costs. Verified by benchmark (1000 notes, 384d): index 16.4MB→8.2MB,
cold-load 49ms→1.1ms (~43x), chunk RAM 24.9MB→6.2MB (4x), and a bit-identical
assert over 1200 pairs reports MAX SCORE DIFF = 0 — ranking results are provably
unchanged.

- Lazy chunk dequant: chunks stay int8 in RAM (4x less); Stage 1 uses only mean
  vectors; a note's chunks are dequantized to fp32 only when it enters the Stage-2
  shortlist, behind an LRU. New src/vector-math.ts holds the dependency-free math
  (also importable by the node benchmark); embeddings re-exports cosineSimilarity.
- Binary index format: a small JSON manifest + one binary blob (adapter.writeBinary)
  instead of base64-in-JSON. Crash-safe (blob before manifest; totalBytes self-heals).
- Lazy keyphrase labels: computed on first card view (snippet shown meanwhile,
  debounced async, persisted, targeted re-render) — not for all notes at build time.
- Scale-aware: adaptive maxChunks for >2000-note vaults; yield-to-UI between embed
  batches so indexing never freezes.
- INDEX_VERSION 3->4: one clean one-time rebuild on upgrade; old JSON index discarded.

bench/at-rest.mjs reproduces the numbers with plain `node`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.5.0: link-building — inline glow + 1-click link, and a semantic [[ suggester

Two precision-first linking features, fully self-contained (reuse the existing
embeddings; no new model), working WITH and WITHOUT the easy-links plugin.

Feature A — inline glow + 1-click link (src/link-glow.ts, src/title-index.ts):
  the FIRST unlinked mention of a concept that already has a note (matched by
  EXACT title/alias text via a longest-first, Unicode-boundary alternation over a
  title+alias index — so a hit is always a real note) glows in live preview; click
  converts it to [[Note]] (or [[Note|Alias]]). A "Link all unlinked mentions"
  command and an opt-in idle auto-link-of-subsequent-mentions setting. Skips
  existing links/code/frontmatter and the note's own title; per-view scoped for
  split panes; no rebuild on selection moves.
Feature B — smart [[ suggester (src/link-suggester.ts): ranks existing notes by
  SEMANTIC relevance to the cursor's paragraph (blended 60/40 with the typed-query
  fuzzy match), excludes the active note, and offers clearly-labelled "create new
  note" rows for strongly-relevant concepts that don't exist yet. Coexists with
  easy-links (take-over defaults OFF when easy-links' smart suggester is on, honours
  an explicit user toggle) and works standalone.

CodeMirror 6 (@codemirror/view, @codemirror/state) added as externalized devDeps.
Validated live: title index resolves, glow decorations render (llm/analysis), 12
suggesters registered, no load errors.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.4.1: ground empty/stub notes in their title; stop labels echoing the title

Live-measured: a title-only note (empty body) ranked off the single luckiest
chunk match — directionalMax takes a MAX, so a one-word title (e.g. "Ableiten")
spuriously aligned with some passage and surfaced under an unrelated topic.

- biMax: a stub (chunkCount === 1) is now scored by the cosine of its title to the
  OTHER note's normalized mean vector (its overall topic) instead of the best
  single chunk. So an empty note's relevance comes mainly from its title vs the
  active note's overall meaning, as it should. Notes with any body use full
  bidirectional MaxSim unchanged. (No re-embed needed — works on stored vectors.)
- Summary label: empty/stub notes (and any label that just echoes the title) now
  leave the line blank instead of repeating the card name. Suppressed at display
  time too, so older indexes that stored the basename are fixed on reload without
  a rebuild.

Verified live: empty math stubs dropped ~11 points and several fell off; the
directly-related notes hold the top; no more title-echo label lines.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.4.0: concise 3-7 word topic labels + drop the same-folder signal

- Summary line is now a tight KeyBERT-style topic LABEL instead of a full
  sentence: candidate 1-3-gram phrases (sentence-bounded, DE+EN stopword/number
  filtered, German mid-sentence-capitalised-noun preference) are embedded in one
  pass with the existing model, ranked by cosine to the note mean, MMR-diversified
  (λ=0.6), and assembled into a 3-7 word label. Computed at index time, persisted,
  cached by mtime. No new model/download. Falls back to the title for tiny notes.
- Removed the "same folder" structural signal + "Same folder" pill: in an atomic
  vault with a handful of folders it fired for almost everything. SIGNAL_NORM
  re-derived from links + shared tags + co-citation + frontmatter.
- INDEX_VERSION 2 -> 3: one automatic full rebuild on upgrade, then labels load
  from disk.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.3.0: chunk-level embeddings + BiMax ranking + structural boost (real quality fix)

Diagnosed live: whole-note mean-pooling made long notes blur together —
cosine(security essay, unrelated German Bio note)=0.808 OUTRANKED cosine(security
essay, directly-related Proofwork)=0.714, because averaging a long varied note
regresses to a generic centroid. Paraphrase models are trained on sentences;
feeding them whole essays misuses them.

Redesign (schema v1 -> v2, forces a one-time full reindex on upgrade):
- Each note is now embedded as a TITLE chunk (weighted 2x) + capped sentence-window
  body chunks, not one mean vector.
- Ranking is a 2-stage funnel: Stage 1 a cheap mean-vector shortlist (low recall
  floor only); Stage 2 a symmetric Bidirectional-MaxSim over the two notes' chunk
  sets — two notes match on their BEST-matching chunks, so topical overlap wins and
  the centroid blur is gone. (Verified to invert the measured case.)
- Bounded hybrid structural boost (direct links, shared tags, co-citation/shared
  links, same folder, frontmatter) with an interpretable why-related pill +
  linked/related connection type; tunable "structure influence" (can't promote
  unrelated notes).
- minSimilarity now applies to the FINAL displayed score (Stage 2), not the
  unreliable mean cosine.
- Centroid-sentence summary line (reuses the chunk vectors — no extra download),
  cached per note.
- int8-quantized chunk persistence to keep the index small.

Fully self-contained/offline; existing features (incremental updates, keyword
fallback, model/device swap, profiles, force rebuild) intact.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.2.1: "Rebuild index" now forces a full re-embed (no more instant no-op)

The manual Rebuild reused vectors whose mtime was unchanged, so with the same
model it finished instantly and changed nothing — confusing when a user clicks it
expecting fresh embeddings. build() now takes a `force` flag; rebuildIndex() sets
it so every note is re-embedded with the current model/settings. (A model change
still rebuilds automatically via setEngine clearing the vectors.)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.2.0: default to symmetric similarity models (much better related-note ranking)

The embeddings ranked poorly (e.g. an English security essay scoring 0.81 with
German biology notes, above its own topic siblings) because the default model,
multilingual-e5-small, is a RETRIEVAL model: trained for short-query → document
search, asymmetric, with a compressed/anisotropic passage space that doesn't
discriminate note-to-note similarity. It was also prefixed "passage:" on both
sides; e5's guidance is to use "query:" for non-retrieval tasks.

Changes:
- Default + Balanced model -> paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2, a SYMMETRIC
  sentence-similarity model purpose-built for "are these two texts alike". Best
  quality stays paraphrase-multilingual-mpnet-base-v2 (stronger, larger). e5 kept
  as an option, relabelled as retrieval/weaker-for-similarity.
- e5 prefix: "passage:" -> "query:" on both sides (symmetric; no-op for the
  prefix-free paraphrase models).
- Updated Model setting copy + README accordingly.

Existing installs keep their saved model until the user switches; applying a
profile or picking a model rebuilds the index.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.1.6: patch BOTH Node checks in the served ort glue (the real worker_threads fix)

1.1.5 served a patched glue via Blob but only patched ONE of the glue's two Node
checks. Proof it was actually loaded: the 1.1.5 error was `import('worker_threads')`
(the worker-bootstrap check) and NOT `import('module')` (the process.type check's
path) — so the process.type patch HAD taken effect; a second, separate check was
still firing.

The bundled ort-web loads its jsep glue EXTERNALLY (not inlined), and that glue has
two independent Node gates, each importing the Node-only 'worker_threads' at module
load:
  1. n = ... && "renderer" != process.type        -> import("module") + require("worker_threads")
  2. typeof globalThis.process?.versions?.node == 'string'  -> await import('worker_threads')

gen-ort now forces BOTH false in the patched glue we serve via Blob URL
(wasmPaths = { mjs: blob, wasm: <local-or-CDN .wasm> }), so the glue loads clean on
the main thread and takes the pure-web path. Reverted the brief main.js post-build
patch attempt (the glue isn't inlined, so the needle wasn't there).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.1.5: serve a web-patched ort glue so it never imports worker_threads

process.type is read-only in Electron, so v1.1.4's attempt to set it to
"renderer" silently no-op'd and onnxruntime-web's glue kept taking the Node path:
`n = ... && "renderer" != process.type` was true, so it did
`await import("module")` + `require("worker_threads")` → "Failed to resolve module
specifier 'worker_threads'" / "no available backend found".

Fix that doesn't depend on the environment at all: gen-ort.mjs now emits a PATCHED
copy of the jsep glue with that one check replaced by `!1`, so its
ENVIRONMENT_IS_NODE is always false and the worker_threads / node-fs branches are
dead. embeddings serves this patched glue to ort via a Blob URL
(wasmPaths = { mjs: blobURL, wasm: <local-or-CDN .wasm> }); the glue then takes
the pure-web path — fetch the wasm, instantiate, run single-threaded on the main
thread. Works for BRAT installs (CDN .wasm) and zip installs (local .wasm) alike.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.1.4: set process.type="renderer" so ort's wasm glue takes the web path

Live console diagnosis: crossOriginIsolated=false (so threading was never on, and
numThreads was a red herring), yet onnxruntime-web's Emscripten glue still
imported the Node-only 'worker_threads' on the MAIN thread.

The glue decides node-vs-web with `process.versions.node && process.type
!= "renderer"`. In Obsidian's renderer process.type is NOT "renderer", so the
glue concluded Node and `import()`ed 'worker_threads' → "Failed to resolve module
specifier 'worker_threads'" / "no available backend found". This is a SECOND,
independent Node check from transformers' IS_NODE_ENV (which we already handle via
process.release.name), it runs at session-create time, and it reads process.type.

Fix: ort-shim sets process.type = "renderer" (its correct value in an Electron
renderer) persistently, before any embedding runs, so the glue takes the web path
and loads the wasm via fetch — no worker_threads, no Node module resolution.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.1.3: set wasm numThreads=1 on the object ort actually reads (fixes worker_threads)

After the web backend started loading, ort spawned a pthread worker that tried to
`import('worker_threads')` (a Node-only module) → "Failed to resolve module
specifier 'worker_threads'" / "no available backend found".

Cause: ort reads its wasm flags from ort.env.wasm, which the WEB runtime exposes
as `env.backends.onnx.env.wasm`. configureEnv only set `env.backends.onnx.wasm`
(undefined in the web build), so numThreads=1 never applied — and because
Obsidian's renderer is cross-origin isolated, ort defaulted to multi-threading
and spawned the worker. (In the Node build the shape is the opposite:
env.backends.onnx.wasm exists, env.backends.onnx.env does not.)

Fix: set wasmPaths + numThreads=1 + proxy=false on BOTH env.backends.onnx.wasm
and env.backends.onnx.env.wasm, so the object ort reads is always hit. Single
threaded, no proxy worker → no worker_threads, runs inline on the main thread
(process.type === "renderer", so the glue uses the web path).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.1.2: take the real web backend by flipping IS_NODE_ENV (not a symbol shim)

Live diagnosis in Obsidian's devtools showed the true cause. transformers.js
v3 builds its backend exactly once, at import time:

    if (Symbol.for("onnxruntime") in globalThis) ONNX = globalThis[that]   // sets runtime, NEVER fills the device list
    else if (IS_NODE_ENV) { ...push("cpu") }                               // node: only cpu
    else { ...push("webgpu"?, "wasm") }                                    // web: the correct list

The previous Symbol.for("onnxruntime") shim made transformers use ort-web (so
"create of undefined" went away) but took the FIRST branch, which leaves
supportedDevices empty — hence every device failed with "Should be one of: .".

IS_NODE_ENV is `process?.release?.name === "node"`, evaluated once at import.
ort-shim.ts now flips process.release.name to a non-"node" value just before
transformers imports, then restores it on the next microtask. transformers then
takes the real WEB branch: bundled onnxruntime-web AND a populated device list
(webgpu when available, else wasm). No global symbol left behind.

Removed the now-dead onnxruntime-web.d.ts and the Symbol.for install.

NOTE: upgrading from 1.0.x/1.1.0/1.1.1 requires a full Obsidian restart once —
the old build's globalThis symbol persists in the renderer across plugin
reloads, so a fresh window is needed to clear it.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.1.1: fall back to CDN wasm when the ort/ folder isn't installed

BRAT and manual installs copy only main.js + manifest.json + styles.css from
the release assets — NOT the ort/ folder (that ships only inside
related-notes.zip). configureWasmPaths() derived the wasm URL with
getResourcePath(), which returns an app:// URL whether or not the file exists,
then set it unconditionally. So on a BRAT install the wasm path 404'd, ORT
registered zero execution providers, and every embed failed with
"Unsupported device: wasm. Should be one of: ." — never falling back to the CDN.

Fix: check adapter.exists() for the probe wasm first; only self-host when it's
actually present. When absent, leave the base unset so the engine uses its
version-pinned jsdelivr fallback (verified reachable through Obsidian's CSP:
HTTP 206). The full zip still self-hosts and runs offline.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.1.0: rename to Smart Related Notes + fix on-device embeddings init

The real root cause of "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading
'create')": in the Electron renderer, transformers.js detects a Node env
(process.release.name === "node") and takes the onnxruntime-NODE branch, but
that package is externalized, so InferenceSession is undefined and webgpu/wasm
are rejected as unsupported (only the Node ["cpu"] provider list was built).

Fix: src/ort-shim.ts installs our bundled onnxruntime-web under
Symbol.for("onnxruntime") BEFORE @huggingface/transformers is imported (it's
imported first in main.ts). transformers checks that symbol ahead of its
Node/Web detection, so the renderer now uses the real web runtime —
InferenceSession is defined and the device list is rebuilt as [webgpu?, wasm].

- Remove the invalid "cpu" device (wasm IS the CPU path for ort-web);
  DevicePref is now auto|webgpu|wasm and every order ends in wasm.
- Self-host the ort .wasm via an app:// URL from the plugin folder, with a
  version-pinned jsdelivr fallback, so it runs offline and the glue/wasm match.
- Rename to "Smart Related Notes" (id stays "related-notes"); view title and
  ribbon now read "Smart related notes".
- Rewrite README to lead with the on-device / private / offline story.

Fully self-contained: no server, no second app.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
v1.0.1: fix device error (native CPU), slider values, 20s re-embed, profiles

- Device: Electron loads onnxruntime-node, which rejects webgpu/wasm (the
  'Unsupported device' error). Route to native CPU there (fast); every device
  order ends in 'cpu' so init can't dead-end. Added a 'cpu' option.
- Settings sliders now show their numeric value.
- Re-embed a changed note only after 20s of edit-idle (not while typing).
- Add Balanced / Best quality one-click performance profiles.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
ci: fix wasm-verify step (onnxruntime-web blocks require of its package.json)

Read the pinned ORT version from the generated src/ort-version.ts instead.
